pi_items.verified_value: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan Projects\Contemporary House.ddp (or similar Contemporary House file)
pi_items.source: Environment setup scripts (setup_dreamplan.ps1 lines 233-236, task_utils.ps1 lines 489-550) explicitly reference loading 'Contemporary House' sample project, not 'Sample House'. Multiple task.json files in the same environment (change_flooring_material, complete_home_staging, renovation_material_package, etc.) directly reference 'Contemporary House' in their descriptions. The create_backyard_patio task.json shows project_path as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\DreamPlan Projects\Sample House.ddp' with .ddp extension; change_room_ceiling_height validated_pi.json confirms DreamPlan uses .ddp as native format, not .dp.

DreamPlan by NCH Software (https://www.nchsoftware.com/design/) supports Roof tools and Accessories library for exterior elements. However, no public documentation, help files, or other tasks in this environment confirm the availability of a 'Chimney' object as a pre-built element in DreamPlan's object library. WebSearch results for NCH DreamPlan documentation (help.nchsoftware.com) show Roof Building features but do not list Chimney among available accessories. The verifier.py requires VLM detection of 'brick chimney' in final screenshots but provides no evidence the object exists in the software.

Multiple verified_pi.json files from other tasks in the same environment confirm Brick is an available exterior material in DreamPlan. Specifically: change_exterior_siding validated_pi.json states 'The software supports brick as an exterior wall material option as documented in the official tutorial.' add_backyard_gazebo validated_pi.json references stone, flagstone, paver, brick, and slate as 'legitimate hardscape materials' in DreamPlan.

pi_items.verified_value: Roof (main roof slope of the house)
pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'Place the chimney on the main roof slope of the house. Ensure it is attached to the roof and not floating or on the ground.' This is consistent with how DreamPlan's Roof tool and accessories work. The verifier.py checks 'Is the chimney correctly placed on the roof (not floating in sky, not on the grass)' confirming this is the intended location specification.

Verified by reading setup_dreamplan.ps1 and task_utils.ps1 which implement the DreamPlan environment setup. The environment launches NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software on Windows 11 with the Contemporary House sample project loaded.

File system check shows that setup_task.ps1 does NOT exist in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/dreamplan_home_design_software_env/tasks/install_roof_chimney/. The only files present are: task.json, verifier.py, and __pycache__/. Other tasks in the same environment (change_flooring_material, complete_home_staging, etc.) DO have their setup_task.ps1 files. This setup script is missing.

File system check shows that export_result.ps1 does NOT exist in benchmarks/cua_world/environments/dreamplan_home_design_software_env/tasks/install_roof_chimney/. Other tasks that have post_task hooks (complete_home_staging, renovation_material_package, second_floor_addition, etc.) DO have their export_result.ps1 files. This export script is missing.

verifier.py file exists at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/dreamplan_home_design_software_env/tasks/install_roof_chimney/verifier.py and contains function verify_install_roof_chimney (lines 20-124). The verifier uses VLM to check: (1) File modified status via C:\tmp\task_result.json (20 points), (2) Menu/tool navigation to Roof/Accessories (20 points), (3) Chimney visible in final screenshot (30 points), (4) Brick material (15 points), (5) Placement on roof (15 points). Pass threshold is 70 points.

privileged_info_summary: DreamPlan by NCH Software is a real commercial home design application. The task requires installing a brick chimney on the roof of a sample house project. CRITICAL ISSUES: (1) The task metadata specifies project_path as 'Sample House.dp', but the environment actually loads 'Contemporary House' as the sample project, and DreamPlan's native format is .ddp, not .dp. (2) Required setup files are missing: setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 do not exist in the task directory, though the task.json hooks reference them. These files are present for other similar tasks in the environment. (3) The 'Chimney' object cannot be verified as an available element in DreamPlan's library—no public documentation or other task files confirm its existence as a pre-built object. The verifier relies on VLM to detect a brick chimney in screenshots (pass threshold 70 points) but provides no evidence the object can be placed. Brick as an exterior material is confirmed available in DreamPlan. The task difficulty is marked 'medium' with 300-second timeout, 40-step maximum, and sparse rewards.
